content_markdown: Two different objects are in thermal contact with one another. The objects are at different temperatures. The temperatures of the two objects determine

diagram_metadata.description: No diagram provided in the image.

options.label: A
options.text: the process by which thermal energy is transferred.

options.label: B
options.text: the heat capacity of each object.

options.label: C
options.text: the direction of transfer of thermal energy between the objects.

options.label: D
options.text: the amount of internal energy in each object.

vibe_explanation: By definition, thermal energy (heat) flows spontaneously from a region of higher temperature to a region of lower temperature. While internal energy and heat capacity are related to temperature, the temperature gradient specifically dictates the direction of the net energy transfer.
